name: Fruitcake in a Jar
recipeInstructions:
Use wide mouth pint size canning jars; do not use mayonnaise jars, etc.
Ensure jars and tops are sterilized according to manufacturer's directions.
Grease the inside of jars but not the rim.
Dredge raisins and/or nuts in some flour. Cream together sugar and shortening.
Beat in eggs and water. Add fruit. Sift together flour, ground cloves, cinnamon,
baking powder, baking soda, and salt. Add to batter if desired; mix in raisins
and/or nuts.
Note: The two cups of fruit are up to you. Use any mixture of fruit but do not use
more than two cups. Two cups grated apple + 1/2 cup applesauce and 1/2 cup pineapple,
or 2 cups shredded carrots, mashed bananas, etc.
Pour batter into sterilized jars (1 measuring cup per jar). Do not use more or the cake will overflow; jars will not seal.
Place jars evenly spaced apart on a cookie sheet for baking. Place in preheated
325-degree oven.
Bake about 45 minutes, or until toothpick inserted in center comes out clean.
Remove jars one at a time using two potholders (jars will be very hot). Leave other jars in the oven but work quickly.
Wipe rim. Place metal disc on top and secure with screw ring; you will hear it
seal with a "ping" sound quite fast.
Open oven, remove another jar, repeat. Generally, any quick bread recipe works,
but ensure one cup of batter per jar. The cake should slide out whole or can be cut into sticks.
The cake should last for one year.
